subject: Accounting
title: The Cost of Speaking Up
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Cost of Speaking Up.

hook.text: Many whistleblowers lose their jobs, their friends, and their mental health. Some never recover.

buildup.text: Retaliation is common—demotion, isolation, harassment, and termination.

discovery.text: Studies show that over half of whistleblowers face some form of retaliation from their employer.

twist.text: Despite legal protections, enforcement is slow and whistleblowers often carry the burden for years.

climax.text: The personal cost explains why most people who see wrongdoing stay silent.

punchline.text: Speaking up costs. Staying silent costs more—just not to you.

quiz.question: What happens to many whistleblowers?
options: They face retaliation like demotion or termination
